reviews.review_title: Use mouse left click to pass load screen
reviews.review_description:
READ THIS IF YOU GET STUCK ON THE LOADING/TITLE SCREEN:
There is a bug where the loading/title screen asks you to hold the trigger to continue, but nothing happens. To get around this, you can connect an external mouse, either via Bluetooth or USB, and then press left-click, instead of trigger. After that you can play the whole game no problem. There is just one other section where you will need to use mouse left-click again (when they ask you to press trigger to blow wind). But the other menus use controller trigger like normal.

The game/experience itself is very nice, like a high quality experimental documentary animation but more interactive and immersive.
It says to press something specific and I’m doing it but it won’t work can you please fix it.
Cant get past title screen and holding trigger does nothing this game sucks
Doesn't go past the title screen

Says
 "hold the trigger." holding it does nothing.

This is an incredible experience that attempts to give us some idea of the world of blindness. It is beautifully made and very poignant. I actually remember this from the Gear VR back in 2016 and I'm delighted to see it here. To the powers that be at Meta : There is another beautiful experience called Annie Amber  that I remember from the Gear VR.....if you could bring it back, it would be wonderful !😊
Cant start
never gets past the opening screen
I've only just started playing the game, but it is wonderful. I find the way that the story is portrayed extremely clever. I can't believe that this game is free! I am excited to go and play more.
Touching real life story and an insight on blindness and what blind people really see around them. Just with their ears, they see more than sighted people.

This is amazing. I never knew what it would’ve been like being blind until I saw Notes on Blindness. And because of that I learned that being blind is not that bad. Being blind means It increases your other senses. (Mostly hearing) I now sometimes walk around the house FEELING how good I would be if I went blind one day. I recommend it for all people of all ages. Have fun!!!
I didn't know how something could make me understand blindness and was skeptical of this title but WOW. A MUST EXPERIENCE in VR. Very personal and moving, yet so simple.

I’m amazed, this is a very good depiction on what blindness is like. It made me realize without sight all other senses and emotions are heightened. I’ve never had a game almost make me cry before. For that I have to correct myself. This isn’t a game, it’s a masterpiece. 🙌

We take things for granted, the perception of the unknown, what to expect, the fear, panic, anxiety takes over every situation. You can hear but can't see what is surrounding you. Just can't imagine... That you never would know how your love one's  might look like. We complain about any simple things in life. That you're not looking the other person is going through something worse than you. Life goes on a blink of an eye. We need to cherish everything the Lord gives us. Be happy for who you are and never think that you're the only that has prove and circumstances that you can deal with. Thank you for showing me to see life differently.  I cried at the end 😭  very emotional 😢 this is the best I have seen in the VR true life events. God bless everyone 💙❤

I didn't have high hopes for this but gave it a go as it was free and I'm glad I did as I found it a very rewarding experience. The narrator communicates extremely well and to be honest, it would make a fascinating documentary even without VR but I do think the VR experience took it to another level. I guess I'd never really considered what it would be like to be blind and this made me think about things I had never really considered - always a good thing! I recommended it to a friend who wasn't wowed by it so perhaps not for everyone but 5 stars from me.
An incredibly innovative VR experience that takes you on a moving journey. Absolutely beautiful. Highly recommended.
Truly amazing in simulating the blind experience. It’s so poetic and beautiful. With the narrative you really feel like you are in that person’s world. Feeling how he feels.

Really a beautiful experience, full of imagination and reality and poetry. Unlike any experience I’d ever think I would have, and an incredible foray into what kinds of journeys we all can share on this miraculous device. Seeing the sound, and influencing it, existing within it, makes one envision just what someone with a different perception than most of us may be like, if only for a moment. Breathtaking.

reviews.review_title: Not bad but not entertaining at all
reviews.review_description:
The start what do you do to have fun just sit there and watch 

Ps not fun

I like to think of myself as a deep thinker but the only thing that I could think of while experiencing this was how bored I was. You stand around listening to park sounds while the blind guy tells you what he heard.  People cried while experiencing this? How?  I was ready to take off my headset In the first minute.  I pushed on for 10 mins or so just walking in circles in my garage wanting it to be over. I was trying to make it to the end (if there is one)couldn't though.  Just type in park sounds in youtube put on some headphones on and close your eyes.  Try to visualize the sounds yourself that would prob be deeper then this.  I know this is harsh but this is not even worth the free price tag.  Maybe for some it is try it out, u b the judge.

reviews.review_title: Watch this, your heart will thank you
reviews.review_description: I don't think any vr experience has so completely engrossed me like notes on blindness has. I'm honestly at a loss for words. I went into it expecting, well, nothing really, a bore at best. this is the heart of virtual reality I think. To make people see things from a different angle, and bring their humanity back. Will definitely be showing this to people.
What's going on?
Thank you
The game don't fix! I live in Italy and the game don't fix for me and my friends that have oculus quest! Please fix it

A personal, insightful glimpse into one person's experience of life without vision. I just wish there had been more to this very poignant and unique VR documentary. Seemed very short. Perhaps one day an imaginative developer will come up with a "game-like" application that simulates the experience of being "virtually blind" within a "virtually visual" environment. This documentary gave us a very meaningful, though short, taste of that potential; experiencing a world without vision, to be virtually reliant on all our senses other than sight. Very compelling.
That was absolutely amazing! Thank you. I am also looking forward to letting others experience it too. You did a great job in my opinion.
This is very difficult to explain but it’s an interesting experience.  Well worth it.  You won’t regret it.
Brought tears to my eyes, makes you value things you take for granted.

For this free experience it brought a new perspective to living and the understanding of the world around me. This new prospective allowed me to see the world through the lens of a blind man and how he found beauty and meaning in the sound that surrounded him.
Thank you for the great experience!!! We need to get different views of life and this give me one different „view“ ❤️

reviews.review_title: Notes on Blindness-A Review by R. Gant.
reviews.review_description: This series of video clips take you on a narrated journey that challenges the  significance that most people place on our ability to see. I was particularly amazed at how reflective I became as I delved deeper into this narrative. John's description of his transition from a "sighted" to a "non-sighted" person is riveting and, without a doubt,  makes for  one of the most moving virtual reality experiences I've encountered.

Such a wonderful way to tell a story.  Using audio tapes from the 1970s paired with the potential of VR this experience both delights as well as informs.  Using emotion, science and art the viewer will gain insights on what it is like to be blind.

THIS is the future!
Really is an amazing experience but couldn’t help feeling some what guilty of the way I was experiencing it..
Simply put, this game is more a beautiful work of art that you get to experience via VR.  If you are one who who prefers the more peaceful VR experiences, definitely check this out.

This was the most meaningful and beautiful VR experience I've ever had.  VR games are fun, but this type of experience can change the world.  A huge THANK YOU to everyone who took part in making this...you all are incredible and please do more experiences like this.  For those who plan to experience this, please USE HEADPHONES...it enhances the experience quite a bit.

reviews.review_title: Exceptional! It will open your eyes!
reviews.review_description:
This VR Experience is magnificent! It will guide you through words, sounds and sensory input through some experiences that a blind person most likely go through. No gimmicks, no cute tricks... just the truth. I experienced how to "see" with sounds and imagine (in my ignorance) how cool would it be to see with sonar... until I experience the fear, loneliness and isolation of the unknown... then the joy of a different perspective and the magic of sound. At the end I realized that few tears had been shed when I heard myself uttering very softly "Thank you, John."

Now I do not feel piety for the blind, but a (minuscule) understanding that the world is different for them. Sometimes it can be terrifying, others beyond beautiful. I think I'm better prepared to have a conversation with someone that is visually impaired (totally or partially) and do better trying to make a connection, and thus a better world.

Share this VR Experience with family and friends. Grab a chair and enjoy this experience sitting down... your life will change for the better. :)

Thank you Novelab and Atlas V (Developers) for sharing John's recordings with the world. BRAVO OCULUS for supporting such magnificent efforts!
I didnt have the emotional experience that alot of people had that made them cry but it was still a very touching one!!
It's a bit underwhelming of what's happening.

I wasn't expecting much yet I left a better human being than I was going into it. It's based on audio recordings from a  man whom recently went blind. You become immersed into a blind persons life, experiencing the world in a way that your vision basically robs from you. It was a very spiritual experience. Not in a religious sense but in a peaceful awakening that brings enlightenment to the world of the blind, not only does it not make you feel sorry for their handicap but it gives you a longing to experience life the way that they do.

If I had to describe this experience in one word, it would be -unique- but that’s surely not only unique. The experience had inside the story made me feel the world another way now. I can only say thank you for such an amazing masterpiece.
